description: Implement an automated system that periodically checks the validity of all resource links (books, courses, casts) across all language Markdown files and generates reports for broken links. This will help maintain the repository's reliability by identifying outdated resources and allowing contributors to fix them efficiently.
functional_requirements: Create a Python script in a new `scripts/` directory that parses all `.md` files in `books/`, `casts/`, and `courses/` to extract URLs.
Implement asynchronous HTTP requests to check each link's HTTP status code, handling timeouts and redirects appropriately.
Generate a weekly report in Markdown format (`reports/link-health-YYYY-MM-DD.md`) listing broken links by file, including the line number and resource title.
Integrate the script into GitHub Actions CI to run on a scheduled basis (e.g., weekly) and automatically open issues for files with new broken links.
Add configuration options (e.g., timeout thresholds, retry limits) via a `config.yaml` file in the repository root.
Update `CONTRIBUTING.md` to document the new automated checking process and how contributors can use reports to fix links.
